title: helietta trifoliata
intro: Helietta trifoliata is a plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the rue or citrus family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. Helietta trifoliata is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically made by steeping the leaves or bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
